title: pyosys interface for Pass doesn't work with virtual functions.
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

With version `Yosys 0.9+3905 (git sha1 4e741add, gcc 9.3.0-17ubuntu1~20.04 -fPIC -Os)` and `PYOSYS_ENABLE=1`.

With this python file, run as `python3 pass.py`

File: `pass.py`
```py
from pyosys import libyosys as ys


class MyPass(ys.Pass):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__("my_pass",  "Runs my pass")
        ys.log("Init on MyPass\n")

    def py_help(self):
        ys.log("Runs my pass\n")

    def py_execute(self, args, design):
        ys.log_header(design, "Running my pass\n")

    def py_clear_flags(self):
        ys.log("Clear Flags - MyPass\n")

    def py_on_register(self):
        ys.log("MyPass on_register called\n")

    def py_on_shutdown(self):
        ys.log("MyPass on_shutdown called\n")


# This should add the instance to the queued_passes.
MyPass()

# This should register the queued_passes into the pass_register.
ys.Pass.init_register()
ys.log("Ready to run MyPass\n")
design = ys.Design()

ys.run_pass("my_pass", design)
```

## Expected behavior

We should see some log messages related running the pass.

## Actual behavior

The output is:

```
Init on MyPass
Ready to run MyPass

-- Running command `my_pass' --
pure virtual method called
terminate called without an active exception
Aborted
```

## Discussion

This apparently happens when `Pass::execute` is called, based on some additional logs I put in `kernel/register.cc`. That's odd because the Python wrappers are supposed to handle pure virtuals. In addition, when `pre_execute` is called, that is supposed to call `clear_flags`. The Python wrappers map this to `py_clear_flags`, which means I should see a `Clear Flags - MyPass` message, which also doesn't appear.

I don't know enough about Boost.Python to know what is going wrong here, but it seems that the whole handling of virtual functions doesn't seem to work.
